REFERENCE: The reference is incomplete (missing title, volume, publication date) and has formatting issues with the volume/issue. While the authors and DOI appear legitimate, the missing critical elements and ambiguous formatting reduce overall reliability. A known Cell review by these authors on metabolism exists from 2021, but the incomplete citation format makes full verification difficult.
authors: These are all well-established researchers in metabolism and diabetes research. Pillon, Loos, Marshall, and Zierath are recognizable names in this field.
title: The reference lacks a title. It is not possible to verify authenticity without title information, but the author combination and journal are consistent with known publications in Cell.
journal_name: Cell is a prestigious, well-known journal. However, the cited volume/issue '(8):310-344' is suspicious. Cell publishes weekly issues but typically with much shorter page ranges per issue. Page range 310-344 is plausible but the issue number '(8)' with such a short range warrants caution.
journal_volume: The volume '(8)' is unclear. Cell issues are typically numbered, but the formatting here is ambiguous and lacks a clear volume designation.
journal_issue: The issue number appears to be 8, but the formatting is unclear without volume context.
journal_page: Pages 310-344 - the range seems reasonable for a Cell article but cannot be fully verified.
elocator: Missing - Cell articles typically have an elocator or article number in newer publications.
publication_date: The publication date is missing from the reference.
doi: The DOI format 10.1016/j.cell.2021.02.012 follows Cell's standard DOI structure (publisher 10.1016, journal cell, year 2021, article identifier 02.012). This format is consistent with legitimate Cell publications from February 2021.
pmcid: PMCID PMC9191863 - follows the standard PMC ID format.
